key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 funds from operations $1.50/sh per PSK-2025-YE-MDA.pdf; Q1 2026 run-rate $0.41/sh ($1.64 annualized)
4% per-share FFO growth (6% oil royalty CAGR offset by commodity mix); exit 18x; dividend $1.06 sustained

open_questions: First deep dive from batch onboard 2026-06-03; verify PFIC treatment for US taxable accounts.
Confirm CAD price source vs TSX close on review date.
Undeveloped acreage overlay left at explicit zero in base; human may size NAV overlay separately.
thesis: PrairieSky Royalty Ltd. is Canada's largest passive oil and natural gas royalty company. **[Fact]** It collects cash from third-party operators on **18.6 million acres** of royalty lands (**9.9 million** fee-simple mineral title acres plus **8.7 million** gross overriding royalty acres) without drilling wells, paying operating costs, or bearing abandonment liabilities (`PrairieSky-Royalty-Presentation-April-2026-POST-AGM.pdf`). **[Fact]** FY2025 funds from operations were **$353.0 million** (**$1.50** per share) on royalty production averaging **25,855** barrels of oil equivalent per day; dividends declared were **$1.04** per share (`PSK-2025-YE-MDA.pdf`). **[Fact]** Q1 2026 funds from operations were **$94.9 million** (**$0.41** per share) on **26,293** BOE per day; net debt was **$257.7 million** with **232.4 million** shares outstanding (`2026-Q1-MDA-FINAL.pdf`). **[Fact]** The company employs roughly **70** Calgary-based staff and returns essentially all excess cash via dividends and opportunistic share repurchases.
why_market_wrong: The market may price PrairieSky as a mature Western Canadian yield stock and underweight the perpetual undeveloped acreage option embedded in fee-simple mineral title. **[Inference]** Third-party spuds on PrairieSky lands totaled **201** wells in Q1 2026 (Clearwater, Mannville Stack, Duvernay, Basal Quartz), with oil royalty production up **6%** year over year while the company carries **zero** operating capital spending. **[Inference]** US and PFIC tax friction (15% Canadian withholding, Form 8621) may keep some yield-focused US accounts on the sidelines despite a **~3.1%** cash dividend at today's price and a conservative **65%** payout ratio in Q1 2026.
